template.description: Default template for newly created C++ source files
template.name: Default C++ source template

template.description: Default template for newly created C++ test files
template.name: Default C++ test template

template.description: Default template for newly created C++ header files
template.name: Default C++ header template

template.description: Default template for newly created C source files
template.name: Default C source template

template.description: Default template for newly created C header files
template.name: Default C header template

template.description: Default template for newly created assembly files
template.name: Default assembly template

template.description: Default template for newly created text files
template.name: Default text file template

templates.template:
#! /usr/bin/scons -u # ----------------------------------------------------------------------- # @attention # Copyright © ${year}, LOCKHEED MARTIN CORPORATION as an unpublished work. # All rights reserved. This computer software is PROPRIETARY INFORMATION of # Lockheed Martin Corporation and shall not be reproduced, disclosed or used # without written permission of Lockheed Martin Corporation. # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# The 'buildUtil' module contains helper utilities to ensure that certain steps # in our build process are consistent across all modules that we will build. import buildUtil import os.path
# IMPORTANT - Always use a standard construction environment env = buildUtil.GetEnv()
# Get compiler flags for our dependencies. # Note: Only *direct* dependencies need be listed here. # See AddFlagsForDepends() documentation in buildUtil.py for more info. #buildUtil.AddFlagsForDepends(env, [ 'lib1', 'lib2' ])
# Add compiler flags that are required by this module. Note: The # AddFlagsForDepends() call above will add flags needed for those dependencies. #env.AppendUnique( CPPDEFINES = ['FOO', 'BAR=13' ] ) # -DFOO -DBAR=13
# Define the name of the lib/jar/executable that will be built. Default to use # the name of the directory where the app is stored (ie, the tail of the PWD). target = os.path.basename(os.getcwd())
# Build an executable from all of the .cpp files and install it app = env.Program(target = target, source = Glob('*.cpp'))
buildUtil.InstallBin(env, app)

#! /usr/bin/scons -u # ----------------------------------------------------------------------- # @attention # Copyright © ${year}, LOCKHEED MARTIN CORPORATION as an unpublished work. # All rights reserved. This computer software is PROPRIETARY INFORMATION of # Lockheed Martin Corporation and shall not be reproduced, disclosed or used # without written permission of Lockheed Martin Corporation. # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# The 'buildUtil' module contains helper utilities to ensure that certain steps # in our build process are consistent across all modules that we will build. import buildUtil
# IMPORTANT - Always use a standard construction environment env = buildUtil.GetEnv()
# Get compiler flags for our dependencies. # Note: Only *direct* dependencies need be listed here. # See AddFlagsForDepends() documentation in buildUtil.py for more info. #buildUtil.AddFlagsForDepends(env, [ 'lib1', 'lib2' ])
# Add compiler flags that are required only for this module. Note: The # AddFlagsForDepends() call above will add flags needed for those dependencies, # so it should rarely be necessary to manually add flags as shown below. If it # is necessary, and if the flags should also be used to compile anything that # links with this library (eg, -D defines that are used in headers), then the # flags must also be passed in to the DefineDependency() call below. See SCons # manpage for documentation of the variables listed below. #env.AppendUnique( CPPDEFINES = [ 'FOO', 'BAR=13' ], # -DFOO -DBAR=13 # CXXFLAGS = [ '-fPIC', '-pipe' ], # add misc C++ compiler flags # CPPPATH = [ '#foo/bar', 'foo/baz' ], # -I/path/to/src/top/level/foo/bar -Ifoo/baz # CCFLAGS = [ '-isystem/usr/foo/include' ], # use -isystem instead of -I for system-level headers that scons should not scan for dependencies (since system-level files rarely change; this helps scons run faster, and> # LIBPATH = [ '/usr/foo/lib' ], # -L/usr/foo/lib # LIBS = [ 'foo' ], # -lfoo # LINKFLAGS = [ '-Wl,-rpath=/usr/foo/lib' ] ) # add misc flags needed by the linker
# Define the name of the lib/jar/executable that will be built. # GetTargetNameFromPWD() just returns the name of the current directory for now. target = buildUtil.GetTargetNameFromPWD()
# Add this module to the build system's dependency database, and define what # compile flags others must use if they depend on this library. See # DefineDependency() documentation in buildUtil.py for more info. buildUtil.DefineDependency(target, env)
# Build a shared library from all of the .cpp files and install it lib = env.SharedLibrary(target = target, source = Glob('*.cpp'))
buildUtil.InstallLib(env, lib)
